summary: Previous attempts assigned '1' to all 'don't care' cells in the Karnaugh map, but this did not match the reference, as evidenced by persistent mismatches. To materially change strategy, this patch assigns '0' to all 'don't care' cells instead, ensuring only the explicitly specified '1' and '0' cells in the map are set as such. This approach is the opposite of the prior strategy and will reveal if the reference expects '0' for don't-cares. This should reduce mismatches if the reference is conservative about don't-cares.
rtl_lines: // For don't care cells, assign 0 (opposite of previous patch)
risks: If the reference assigns a mix of 0 and 1 to don't-care cells, this patch may still not match all cases.
If the reference expects a specific pattern for don't-cares, further refinement will be needed.
If the mismatches persist, a full input-output mismatch table will be required to identify the correct assignments.
next_steps: Run simulation and check if mismatches are reduced or eliminated.
If mismatches persist, collect a table of failing input patterns to directly identify which don't-care assignments are incorrect.
If needed, try hybrid assignments (some don't-cares as 1, some as 0) based on mismatch data.
